algorithms.title: Diabetic Emergency Differentiation Sequence
algorithms.description: Distinguishing hypoglycemia, DKA, and HHNS in the field using glucose, respiratory pattern, and history.
steps: Check blood glucose immediately in any altered mental status or stroke-like presentation.
If glucose is LOW: administer glucose promptly and reassess mental status/deficits for reversal.
If glucose is HIGH: assess the respiratory pattern.
Deep, rapid, labored (Kussmaul) respirations + ketotic breath + hours-to-1-2-day onset + abdominal pain/polyuria/polydipsia → consider DKA.
No Kussmaul pattern + profound dehydration + days-long insidious onset + often older adult → consider HHNS.
Treat as time-sensitive regardless of type: supportive care, transport, and frequent reassessment.
